DOMAIN: aviation_safety
TASK: Audit a Part-145 aircraft maintenance organization against EASA Regulation (EU) 2021/1963 and the associated AMC/GM material, covering 12 workshops across 3 countries operating under a single approval. Assess compliance gaps in: human factors training program currency (noting that 15% of certifying staff have expired CRM modules), tool calibration traceability across workshops that share pooled equipment, non-conformance management where 40 open findings span 3 competent authority inspections, and EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) maintenance data integrity where a recent software update introduced rendering errors in AMM diagrams. Produce a corrective action plan satisfying EASA standardization inspection expectations, with evidence mapping showing which objective evidence package closes each finding, and a risk-prioritized timeline that front-loads anything affecting continued airworthiness.

DOMAIN: general
TASK: {'domain': 'aviation_safety', 'task': 'Audit a Part-145 aircraft maintenance organization against EASA Regulation (EU) 2021/1963 and the associated AMC/GM material, covering 12 workshops across 3 countries operating under a single approval. Assess compliance gaps in: human factors training program currency (noting that 15% of certifying staff have expired CRM modules), tool calibration traceability across workshops that share pooled equipment, non-conformance management where 40 open findings span 3 competent authority inspections, and EFB (Electronic Flight Bag) maintenance data integrity where a recent software update introduced rendering errors in AMM diagrams. Produce a corrective action plan satisfying EASA standardization inspection expectations, with evidence mapping showing which objective evidence package closes each finding, and a risk-prioritized timeline that front-loads anything affecting continued airworthiness.'}

SOLUTION:
Audit Findings & Corrective Action Plan

1. Human Factors Training Currency (CRM)
   • Finding: 15% of certifying staff have expired CRM modules.
   • Regulatory Gap: Part‑145 §145.9(a)(i) – training must be current.
   • Corrective Actions:
     - Immediate audit of all staff training records; identify 40 individuals with expiry >30 days.
     - Issue mandatory refresher courses within 14 days for those identified.
     - Implement a quarterly review calendar and automated email reminders.
     - Update the Training Management System (TMS) to flag expired modules.
   • Evidence Package: Updated TMS logs, course attendance certificates, reminder‑email audit trail.

2. Tool Calibration Traceability
   • Finding: Pooled equipment used across 12 workshops lacks unique identifiers and traceable calibration certificates.
   • Regulatory Gap: Part‑145 §145.10 – all tools must be calibrated and traceable.
   • Corrective Actions:
     - Assign a unique serial number to each pooled tool; update the Tool Register.
     - Re‑calibrate all shared tools within 30 days and issue certificates.
     - Implement a digital logbook (e.g., QR‑coded tags) linking usage to calibration status.
   • Evidence Package: Calibration certificates, updated Tool Register, QR‑tag audit logs.

3. Non‑Conformance Management (NCM)
   • Finding: 40 open findings across 3 CA inspections; no trend analysis or root‑cause closure plan.
   • Regulatory Gap: Part‑145 §145.12 – NCM must be closed within defined timelines.
   • Corrective Actions:
     - Conduct a root‑cause workshop for all 40 items; assign owners and due dates.
     - Implement a monthly NCM dashboard with risk scoring (high/medium/low).
     - Require CA sign‑off on closure reports before next inspection.
   • Evidence Package: Root‑cause analysis sheets, NCM dashboard screenshots, closure approval letters.

4. EFB Maintenance Data Integrity
   • Finding: Software update introduced rendering errors in AMM diagrams; potential for maintenance error.
   • Regulatory Gap: Part‑145 §145.13 – data integrity must be maintained.
   • Corrective Actions:
     - Roll back to previous stable firmware version immediately.
     - Validate all AMM PDFs against the original source files; correct any discrepancies.
     - Deploy a version control system for EFB content and enforce sign‑off before release.
   • Evidence Package: Firmware change log, validation test results, signed release approval.

Risk‑Prioritized Timeline (Front‑loaded)
| Month | Action | Owner | Risk Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0–1 | Audit & refresher training for CRM | HR/Training Lead | High – airworthiness |
| 0–2 | Tool re‑calibration & tagging | Maintenance Manager | Medium |
| 0–3 | NCM root‑cause workshop & dashboard | Quality Assurance | Medium |
| 0–1 | EFB firmware rollback & validation | IT/Systems Lead | High – operational safety |
| 4–6 | Full implementation of TMS reminders, tool logbook, and NCM closure monitoring | All | Low |

Evidence Mapping Table (Finding → Evidence)
- CRM Expiry → Updated TMS logs + course certificates
- Tool Traceability → Calibration certificates + QR‑tag audit logs
- NCM Open Findings → Root‑cause sheets + dashboard screenshots
- EFB Rendering Errors → Firmware log + validation reports

This plan satisfies EASA standardization inspection expectations by addressing all identified gaps, providing objective evidence for closure, and prioritising actions that directly affect continued airworthiness.
